The source map comment (`//# sourceMappingURL=...`) with embedded base64-encoded JSON is standard for source maps generated during build processes.\n   - No reports mention any dynamic code, user input handling, network activity, or data processing that could introduce vulnerabilities.\n\n2. **Anomalies and Suspicious Elements:**\n   - The only unusual element is the embedded source map comment containing base64-encoded JSON data.\n   - All reports note that this is typical for source maps and not malicious, although its presence in production code can sometimes be suspicious if unexpected.\n\n3. **Malware and Security Risks:**\n   -
